zh.lifeSummary: 當Mary Ann Hopffner於1835年5月21日出生在美國俄亥俄州時，她的父親Peter Hephner29歲，母親Ann Mariah Marshall27歲。她於1850年7月25日與Mathew Nugent在美國威斯康辛州武塔加米蕾托 特結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和8個女兒的父母。她於1895年住在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特斯特萊特 利維 堂史普且於1900年住在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特托德 堂史普。她去世於1919年6月16日在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特享年84歲，埋葬在美國明尼蘇達州賀伯特帕克 拉皮茲。

nameMeanings.name: Hopffner
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of Höffner (see Hoffner ). Compare Hoefner .

variants.name: Hoffner
meanings: German (also Höffner): status name for a prosperous small farmer, from an agent noun derivative of Middle Low German huove, a measure of land. See also Huber , compare Hoeffner and Hofner 2.

nameMeanings.name: Mary
meanings: Hebrew: Bitter; Marion is the French form.

variants.name: Marion
meanings: Originally a medieval French diminutive form of Marie , introduced to Britain in the Middle Ages. In some places it was taken as a pet form of Margaret or Margery . As a male name, it is an altered form of the Continental male name Marian, a derivative of Latin Marianus (from Marius ), but is now rarely, if ever, used. It was the birth name of the American film star John Wayne ( 1907–79 ).

variants.name: Marcia
meanings: Often used as a feminine equivalent of Mark , but in fact a feminine form of Marcius, itself a derivative of Marcus . One St Marcia is commemorated in a group with Felix, Luciolus, Fortunatus, and others; another with Zenais, Cyria, and Valeria; and a third with Ariston, Crescentian, Eutychian, Urban, Vitalis, Justus, Felicissimus, Felix, and Symphorosa. None is individually very famous.

nameMeanings.name: Ann
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Variant spelling of Anne . In the U.S. Ann is the more frequent spelling.

historicEvents.title: Remember the Alamo
historicEvents.description: Being a monumental event in the Texas Revolution, The Battle of the Alamo was a thirteen-day battle at the Alamo Mission near San Antonio. In the early morning of the final battle, the Mexican Army advanced on the Alamo. Quickly being overrun, the Texian Soldiers quickly withdrew inside the building. The battle has often been overshadowed by events from the Mexican–American War, But the Alamo gradually became known as a national battle site and later named an official Texas State Shrine.

historicEvents.title: Kirtland Temple Dedicated

historicEvents.description: Although divided as a state on the subject of slavery, Ohio participated in the Civil War on the Union's side, providing over 300,000 troops. Ohio provided the 3rd largest number of troops by any Union state.
historicEvents.urlTitle: Ohio supports the Union side of the Civil War

historicEvents.description: Abraham Lincoln issues Emancipation Proclamation, declaring slaves in Confederate states to be free.

historicEvents.title: The Assassination of James Garfield
historicEvents.description: Garfield was shot twice by Charles J. Guitea at Railroad Station in Washington, D.C. on July 2, 1881. After eleven weeks of intensive and other care Garfield died in Elberon, New Jersey, the second of four presidents to be assassinated, following Abraham Lincoln.

historicEvents.title: Gold for Cash!
historicEvents.description: This Act set a price at which gold could be traded for paper money.

historicEvents.title: The End of Capital Punishment
historicEvents.description: Between the state's entry into the Union and 1906, 27 people were executed by hanging in Minnesota. Public opinion soon shifted against the death penalty in the state and was formally abolished in 1911. Since its abolishment, there have been 23 attempts to reinstate the death penalty, but none of these bills passed the state legislature.

en.lifeSummary: When Mary Ann Hopffner was born on 21 May 1835, in Ohio, United States, her father, Peter Hephner, was 29 and her mother, Ann Mariah Marshall, was 27. She married Mathew Nugent on 25 July 1850, in Little Chute, Outagamie, Wisconsin, United States. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 8 daughters. She lived in Straight River Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States in 1895 and Todd Township, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States in 1900. She died on 16 June 1919, in Hubbard, Minnesota, United States, at the age of 84, and was buried in Park Rapids, Hubbard, Minnesota, United States.

ko.lifeSummary: Mary Ann Hopffner(이)가 1835년 5월 21일 미국 오하이오 주에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Peter Hephner(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Ann Mariah Marshall(은)는 27세였다. 그녀는 1850년 7월 25일 미국 위스콘신 주 우타갬이 리틀 c후트에서 Mathew Nugent(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 여덟 명의 딸을 두었다. 1895년도에 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 스트레이트 라이버 타운시프에서, 1900년도에 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 토드 타운시프에서 살았다. 그녀는 1919년 6월 16일 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드에서 84세의 나이로 사망했으며 미국 미네소타 주 후브바드 박 라피드즈에 묻혔다.

Ela faleceu em 16 de junho de 1919, em Hubbard, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América, com 84 anos, e foi sepultada em Park Rapids, Hubbard, Minesota, Estados Unidos da América.

Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Mathew Nugent 25 июля 1850 г. в местности литл чут, оутагэими, Висконсин, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 8 дочерей. Она жила в местности стреиит ривир тоуншип, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в 1895 году и в местности тодд тоуншип, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в 1900 году. Она умерла 16 июня 1919 г. в местности хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты в возрасте 84 лет и была похоронена в местности парк рапидс, хуббард, Миннесота, Соединенные штаты.
